footer.tagline: Turn the coffee break into a competition.

Landing.metaTitle: Quest of the Roasted Bean — turn the coffee break into a competition
Landing.metaDescription: Track coffee, tea and cocoa at work with NFC tags, leaderboards and badges. Run it free, self-hosted with Docker, or let us host it for you — safely in the EU.
Landing.heroTitle: Turn the coffee break into a competition
Landing.heroBody: Quest of the Roasted Bean tracks coffee, tea and cocoa for the whole team — with NFC scanning, leaderboards and badges. Build a little friendly rivalry around the coffee machine. ☕
Landing.pricingNote: Free to self-host · or hosted from {price}{period}

features.title: Scan & log
features.body: Hold your phone to an NFC tag by the coffee machine — one tap logs the cup. No app to install.

features.title: Leaderboard
features.body: Daily, weekly and all-time ranking — per person and per department. Who's really the office caffeine king?

features.title: Badges
features.body: Unlock fun badges like “Early Bird” and “Cup Master”. Admins create and control the rules.

features.title: Statistics
features.body: Live dashboard with trends, drink distribution and activity over time — in charming pixel style.

steps.title: Set up
steps.body: Create departments and coffee stations in admin, and attach NFC tags to the machines.

steps.title: Scan
steps.body: People hold their phone to the tag and the cup is logged instantly.

steps.title: Compete
steps.body: Follow the leaderboard, collect badges and watch the stats grow.

Landing.selfHostedBody: Run it on your own machine or server with Docker. You own the data.
Landing.hostedBody: We take care of everything. Ready in minutes, with login and automatic updates.
Landing.endBody: Set up Quest of the Roasted Bean for your team today — and find out who really keeps the office running.

Product.metaTitle: Product — Quest of the Roasted Bean
Product.metaDescription: Everything in Quest of the Roasted Bean: NFC scanning, leaderboards, rule-based badges, statistics and full admin control. Compare self-hosted and hosted.
Product.heroTitle: The whole coffee contest, in one little app
Product.heroBody: Quest of the Roasted Bean turns everyday coffee breaks into a light game with points, badges and leaderboards — built for teams that like a bit of competition.

details.title: NFC scanning
details.body: Attach a tag to each coffee machine. One tap with your phone logs the cup — no app, no signing in on the spot. A tag can be locked to one drink, or let the user choose.

details.title: Leaderboard & departments
details.body: Rank individuals and departments — today, this week or all-time. Perfect for a bit of healthy internal rivalry between teams.

details.title: Rule-based badges
details.body: Admins create badges and set the rule: number of cups, distinct drink types, count of one specific drink, or time of day. They're awarded automatically.

details.title: Live statistics
details.body: Dashboard with trends, the split between coffee/tea/cocoa and activity over time — all in real time and in charming pixel style.

details.title: Profiles & avatars
details.body: Each user has a nickname, department and their own picture. Track your own cups, badges and place on the leaderboard.

details.title: Full admin control
details.body: Create departments, stations, NFC tags and badges from one place. Copy tag links straight from admin.

Product.nfcIntro: A Quest of the Roasted Bean tag contains just a web link. The phone opens the link when scanned, and the cup is logged in a second. Same mechanism whether you host it yourself or let us do it.
Product.nfcSelfBody: The tag points to your local address on the company network, e.g.
Product.nfcHostedBody: The tag points to your Quest of the Roasted Bean domain. The right company is recognized automatically from the token:
Product.nfcFootnote: Admin creates the tag and clicks “Copy link” — which always uses the right domain — and writes the link to the tag with a free app like NFC Tools. A QR code with the same link works for phones without NFC.
Product.selfHostedDesc: Run it on your own machine with Docker. Full control and ownership of the data.
Product.hostedDesc: Ready in minutes. No operations, secure login, backup and updates included — hosted in the EU (GDPR).
Product.hostingIntro: Choose the hosted version and we take care of all the operations — secure, up to date and predictable.

hostingBullets.title: Data in the EU
hostingBullets.body: Everything runs in European data centres (Finland). GDPR-friendly — your data never leaves the EU.

hostingBullets.title: Daily backup
hostingBullets.body: Automatic backup every day, so nothing is lost.

hostingBullets.title: Automatic updates
hostingBullets.body: You always get the latest version without lifting a finger.

hostingBullets.title: Secure login
hostingBullets.body: A dedicated account per user and encrypted HTTPS all the way.

hostingBullets.title: Ready in minutes
hostingBullets.body: Your own domain is set up automatically when you sign up.

hostingBullets.title: Grows with the team
hostingBullets.body: From a small team to the whole company — without you noticing the operations.

faq.q: Do users need to install an app?
faq.a: No. NFC scanning just opens a web page in the phone's browser. Those who want to can create a profile to collect their own badges, but you can also log cups without one.
Yes. Because the tags use a regular web link (URL), scanning works on both iPhone and Android — without an extra app or special permissions.

In the self-hosted version: on your own machine/server — you own everything. In the hosted version: safely with us in European data centres (Finland, GDPR-friendly — your data never leaves the EU), with daily backup and updates included.

faq.q: Can we switch from self-hosted to hosted later?
faq.a: Yes. The physical tags then need to be rewritten once because the domain changes, but all functionality is the same.

Download.metaTitle: Download & install — Quest of the Roasted Bean
Download.metaDescription: Run Quest of the Roasted Bean for free on your own machine with Docker. All dependencies are included — follow the five steps.
Download.title: Download & install
Download.intro: Run Quest of the Roasted Bean for free on your own machine or server. Docker packages everything — app, database (PostgreSQL) and all dependencies. You don't need to install Node, a database or anything else.
Download.onlyReq: ✅ Only requirement: Docker. The rest comes ready in the package.
Download.step1Intro: Docker runs the whole app in isolated “containers”. Install it first:
Download.step1Win: Windows / Mac: download Docker Desktop and run the installer.
Download.step1Linux: Linux: install Docker Engine with your package manager.
Download.step2Title: Get Quest of the Roasted Bean
Download.step2Intro: Download the latest release and unpack it, or get it with git:
Download.step2Zip: (Prefer without git? Download the ZIP from the releases page and unpack it into a folder.)
Download.step3Intro: Copy the example file to .env and adjust as needed (port and admin PIN for the first login):
Download.step3EnvExample:
# Port the app should run on
PORT=3000

# First admin user (created on startup)
ADMIN_NICKNAME=GameMaster
ADMIN_PIN=1234

# Database password (can stay as-is for local use)
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=quest-of-the-roasted-bean
Download.step4Intro: This command starts both the app and the database, runs the database setup automatically and adds the default drinks and badges:
Download.step4Note: The first time it downloads the images — that takes a couple of minutes. After that it starts in seconds.
Download.step5Body1: Go to http://localhost:3000 in your browser. Log in as admin with the nickname and PIN from .env, and create your own departments, stations and NFC tags under Admin.
Download.step5Body2: Want colleagues to reach the app from their phones? Use the machine's network address, e.g. http://192.168.1.20:3000.
Download.nfcBody: Create a tag in admin, click “Copy link”, and write the link to the tag with a free app like NFC Tools (choose “Write” → “URL/URI”). Then one tap logs the cup directly. A QR code with the same link works for phones without NFC.
Download.ctaQuestion: Would you rather skip setup and operations?
Download.footnote: Note: these instructions are for the pre-packaged Docker edition, which comes in the next step of the project.
